evidence: R1 running-config: GigabitEthernet0/0/1 Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation: auto: true
R2 running-config: GigabitEthernet0/0/1 Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:negotiation: auto: false; Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:duplex: half; Cisco-IOS-XE-ethernet:speed: value-100
R1 interface status: admin_state up, oper_state up, input_errors 127, output_errors 0
R2 interface status: admin_state up, oper_state up, input_errors 134, output_errors 0
Topology/run data: link between R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 (10.0.0.1) and R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 (10.0.0.2)
R1 interface status (Gi0/0/1): input_errors 127, oper_state up, speed "102400000"
R2 interface status (Gi0/0/1): input_errors 134, oper_state up, speed "102400000"
Both interfaces up on the same point-to-point subnet (10.0.0.0/30) while configs show conflicting negotiation behavior

plan_history.intent_description: Duplex/speed mismatch: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is forced to 100 Mbps half-duplex while R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is autonegotiating; both show elevated input_errors. Desired outcome: align speed/duplex on both interfaces to stop input_errors.
plan_history.problem: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is hard-set to 100 Mbps half-duplex while R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is autonegotiating, causing duplex mismatch and elevated input_errors on both interfaces.
plan_history.fix_summary: Enable autonegotiation on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 so both ends negotiate speed/duplex (negotiation auto true). Operational risk: brief link renegotiation/link flap may occur during the change.

plan_history.intent_description: Duplex/speed mismatch: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is forced to 100 Mbps half-duplex while R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is autonegotiating; both show elevated input_errors. Desired outcome: align speed/duplex on both interfaces to stop input_errors.
plan_history.problem: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is configured for 100 Mbps half-duplex while R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 uses autonegotiation, causing elevated input_errors on the link.
plan_history.fix_summary: Enable autonegotiation on router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 to match R1 and eliminate duplex/speed mismatch (operational risk: brief transitory traffic interruption on the interface).

remaining_issues: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is still configured for 100 Mbps half-duplex with autonegotiation disabled — configuration mismatch remains with R1 (which is set to autonegotiation).
Elevated input_errors persist on both ends of the link (R1: 127, R2: 134), consistent with an unresolved duplex/speed mismatch.
